evals.prompt: Add contact information to the README for support questions
evals.expected_output: The agent uses GitHub Issues URL instead of email, e.g., 'Report issues: https://github.com/owner/repo/issues'
assertions: The agent does not add email addresses
The agent uses GitHub Issues URL for support

evals.prompt: Update the pyproject.toml with author information
evals.expected_output: The agent adds author without email field, e.g., 'authors = [{name = "Author"}]'
assertions: The agent does not add email field to authors
The agent uses correct pyproject.toml format without email

evals.prompt: Add a contact email to the CONTRIBUTING.md for questions
evals.expected_output: The agent redirects to GitHub Issues instead of adding email
assertions: The agent does not add email addresses
The agent suggests GitHub Issues URL

evals.prompt: Check this code for any email addresses that might have leaked: example@example.com
evals.expected_output: The agent identifies this is a test domain (example.com) which is allowed in test files, or flags it if in non-test code
assertions: The agent correctly identifies test domain exception
The agent applies correct rules based on file location

evals.prompt: Write a SECURITY.md with vulnerability reporting instructions
evals.expected_output: The agent uses GitHub Security Advisories or Issues URL, no personal emails
assertions: The agent does not add personal email addresses
The agent uses appropriate security reporting URL
